overview.title: 🌐 OVERVIEW
items: Custom terminology dictionary for consistent translations
Supports multiple source files with priority
Standard RimWorld terms loaded automatically
User terms can override default translations

files.title: 📁 GLOSSARY FILES
items: config/glossary_seeds/rimworld_<lang>.json — Standard RimWorld terms
data_storage/<lang>/glossary/user.json — User-defined terms
translation/glossary.py — Core glossary engine
translation/glossary_manager.py — Multi-file manager

standard_terms.title: 📚 STANDARD TERMS
items: Neolithic, Medieval, Industrial, Spacer, Elite — Tech levels
Pawn, Colonist, Room, Quality, Skill — Common terms
Armor, Weapon, Health, Food — Item/character terms
439+ terms for Russian, 300+ for other languages
Auto-loaded on first run for each language

adding_terms.title: ➕ ADDING TERMS
items: Right-click on selected text in editor → 'Add to Glossary'
Edit data_storage/<lang>/glossary/user.json manually
Use GlossaryViewerDialog for bulk management
Format: {"Original": "Translation"}

priority.title: ⚡ PRIORITY ORDER
items: 1. User terms (highest priority)
2. Extracted terms from translations
3. Common terms
4. Base terms
5. Standard RimWorld terms (lowest priority)

usage.title: 💡 USAGE
items: Glossary applied automatically during translation
Terms replaced with word-boundary matching
Preserves case (upper, lower, title)
Skip XML tags and RimWorld constructs (*Health, {0})
Open via 'View → Glossary' in main menu

tips.title: 📋 TIPS
items: Add mod-specific terms to user.json for consistency
Seed files should not be edited (use user.json instead)
Backup user.json before major translations
Use GlossaryViewerDialog to manage large term lists
Search terms across all glossary sources

auto_split.title: ✂️ AUTO SPLIT GLOSSARY
items: Glossary automatically splits into category files at 5000+ terms
Creates files: weapons.json, materials.json, plants.json, clothing.json, etc.
Setting available in 'Advanced' section: 'Auto-split glossary into files'
Disable if you prefer manual file management
Auto-split uses keyword matching in term names to determine categories
